profile.name: Mary Miller
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; tends to act as the organizer and point person for household finances and paperwork.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate: understands basic budgeting, retirement accounts, and debt tradeoffs, but gets fuzzy on investment mechanics and tax details unless explained plainly.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Conservative in practice—prefers predictable outcomes and gets uneasy with market swings; will accept some growth exposure only after debt and cash-buffer concerns feel under control.
profile.communication_style: Direct but a little rushed; uses qualifiers like "I think" and "probably"; will say "I don’t remember the exact number" and give ranges; asks for a clear next-step list and repeats back key points to confirm.
emotional_tendencies: anxious when discussing monthly cash flow
relief-seeking (wants a plan that feels immediately actionable)
profile.decision_making_style: Checklist-driven: wants to see options, pros/cons, and a sequence; prefers to decide after hearing how it affects monthly payments and emergency cash.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open: willing to share details but needs the advisor to be transparent, avoid jargon, and not push products.
internal_biases: recency bias (recent tight months weigh heavily)
hidden_concerns: fear of falling behind on payments or having a surprise expense with limited cash on hand
worry that self-employment income variability could derail plans
embarrassment about carrying credit card debt despite having investments

profile.name: Thomas Miller
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; more hands-off on day-to-day bills but engaged when discussing investments and big-picture strategy.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: comfortable talking about brokerage accounts and returns; less attentive to budgeting categories and due dates unless prompted.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Mixed: personally more comfortable with market risk than Mary and may resist selling investments to pay debt; however, when stressed he becomes conservative and wants to avoid making a 'wrong move.'
profile.communication_style: Conversational and sometimes tangential; uses fillers ("uh," "you know") and approximations; may interrupt with a question about performance or taxes; occasionally defers with "whatever you think" when the discussion turns to budgeting details.
emotional_tendencies: defensive if he feels blamed for spending or debt
restless when conversations get too granular
profile.decision_making_style: Big-picture first: wants to understand the rationale and expected outcome; prefers a couple of clear scenarios and then will align with Mary if the plan feels fair and realistic.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Neutral-to-positive: tends to trust expertise quickly but will test the advisor with pointed questions to see if answers are consistent.
internal_biases: status quo bias (prefers not to change accounts unless clearly beneficial)
anchoring on account balances rather than monthly cash flow
hidden_concerns: concern about being judged for not tracking spending closely
worry that tightening the budget will feel restrictive or reduce quality of life
fear that selling investments to pay debt could mean missing future gains
